final_decision.enforcement: mandatory documentation, author-time lint only (no runtime schema validation)
final_decision.format: markdown table within `## Output Contract` section in agent body
final_decision.v9_pre_announced_changes: ship in same v9.0.0 release alongside I/O contracts (.md overlay hard removal + deprecated agent name hard errors)

evidence_basis: peer-deep-dive.md (.forge/phase-2-research-answers/peer-deep-dive.md) — 22 frameworks analyzed, recommendation reconciles CrewAI mandatory `expected_output` precedent with Anthropic Multi-Agent Research System prose-only philosophy. ceos-agents sits between markdown-plugin cohort (BMAD, Claude Code, wshobson — 0/7 declare I/O contracts) and runtime-SDK cohort (CrewAI, Pydantic AI, LangGraph — Pydantic-validated). Convention-as-mandatory + author-time-lint is synthesis-level inference, not direct precedent.
phase_4_spec_mandate: Spec must define `## Output Contract` markdown section format: positioned between `## Process` and `## Constraints` in agent body
Spec must specify two markdown tables: (a) Inputs (Section | Source | Required), (b) Outputs (Section produced | When | Required fields)
Spec must enumerate ALL 18 agents with their `## Output Contract` content — not 5, not partial
Spec must handle polymorphism for the 4 `--phase X` agents (analyst, test-engineer, browser-agent, spec-reviewer) by listing per-phase output blocks
Spec must enumerate 5+ new lint scenarios in tests/scenarios/ with section-level `exit 77` SKIP-guards for forward-compat during the v8.0.0 → v9.0.0 transition

Spec must include CLAUDE.md Versioning Policy amendment (resolves the Q8 documented gap) AND Cross-File Invariants amendment (new invariant: every backtick-quoted `## Heading` declared in any agent's Output Contract MUST be referenced by at least one skills/**/SKILL.md)
Spec must include docs/guides/migration-v8-to-v9.md stub per feedback_docs_coverage discipline
Spec must classify the change as MAJOR per Versioning Policy

Spec MUST address the `stack-selector` orphan finding — agents/stack-selector.md is referenced in scaffold/SKILL.md:91 'legacy flow' text but never actually dispatched. Spec author should choose: (a) delete agent + cleanup the SKILL.md reference, (b) implement actual dispatch in 03-scaffold.md, or (c) add a 6th lint scenario `v9-agents-must-be-dispatched.sh` to prevent future orphans (combinable with a or b). Default: option (c) + option (a) — delete dead code AND prevent recurrence.

Spec MUST address the dual dispatch idiom finding — 14 agents use `Task(subagent_type='ceos-agents:X')` strict idiom (validated by PostToolUse hook); 4 agents (backlog-creator, priority-engine, sprint-planner, stack-selector) use prose `Run ceos-agents:X (Task tool, model: Y)` idiom (NOT validated). Spec should either harmonize all 18 to strict idiom OR document that prose idiom is acceptable for orchestration-level skills. Backward-compat preserved either way.
preserved_from_synthesis: Override injector untouched (append-only, structure-blind per Q6)
Validation: static lint via tests/scenarios/ only — no LLM self-validation, no runtime dispatcher check
Versioning Policy amendment in same PR (per Q8 documented gap)

rejected_alternatives.full_pydantic_runtime_validation: rejected — peer evidence shows runtime schema works only when consumer is Python code; ceos-agents consumers are LLMs (reviewer reads fixer's prose output)
rejected_alternatives.no_contracts_status_quo: rejected — section header drift is real risk; author-time lint catches it in CI cheaply
rejected_alternatives.partial_5_agents_optional: rejected — optional contracts drift (CrewAI lesson); two classes of agents = more downstream complexity
